items.prompt: Which set best matches core metadata fields discussed in this part?
choices: Learning rate, batch size, and epochs only
Creators, creation date, update frequency, sources, and version notes
Only row count and file size
items.explain: Metadata covers authorship, timing, freshness, origins, and version history.

items.prompt: A data dictionary should explain each variable’s name, type, description, allowed values when categorical, and units when continuous.
items.explain: Those fields are the standard dictionary checklist in Section 8.2.

items.prompt: Example 8.8’s purchase_amount entry emphasizes which practice?
choices: Recording type, description, allowed values, and units in a structured entry
Replacing the dictionary with a screenshot
items.explain: The JSON-style entry packages type, meaning, constraints, and USD units together.

items.prompt: In one sentence, what does Example 8.5 illustrate about air-quality metadata?
items.rubric: Should mention organization, time period, measurement frequency, and/or external sources.
items.sample_answer: It lists who collected the data, over what period, at what frequency, and which external sources were used.
